description: Affrication and fronting of the velar nasal [ŋ] to the voiced alveolar affricate [dz]. This change involves moving the place of articulation from velar to alveolar and strengthening the consonant from a nasal to an affricate.

languageExamples.description: This is a rare phonetic shift, potentially occurring in contexts of extreme palatalization or as part of a complex chain shift affecting nasal consonants.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
